common.title: QuitMate | Addiction Recovery Community App — Quit Together
common.description: A free, anonymous community app for addiction recovery. 8,000+ people support each other to quit alcohol, gambling, smoking, porn and more. Rated ★4.8 on the App Store.

hero.title: You don't have to quit alone
hero.description: A safe, anonymous community where people in recovery support each other
hero.screenshot-stories-alt: QuitMate addiction recovery app community feed
hero.screenshot-categories-alt: QuitMate addiction recovery app category selection
hero.screenshot-program-alt: QuitMate addiction recovery app program screen
hero.screenshot-habits-alt: QuitMate addiction recovery app habit tracker

intro.title: A supportive social network for addiction recovery
intro.description: Struggling to quit? You're not alone. QuitMate is a safe, anonymous space where people facing similar challenges come together to support each other in overcoming addictions and unhealthy habits.

solution.title: Three pillars that help you stay on track
solution.description: What's hard alone becomes possible with the right structure and support.

pillar1.title: Anonymous & safe
pillar1.description: No real name or photo required. Share honestly in a judgment-free space.

pillar2.title: People who get it
pillar2.description: Connect with others facing the same addiction. Get real empathy and encouragement.

pillar3.title: See your progress
pillar3.description: Track your clean time. Every hour and day adds up, keeping you motivated.

feature1.title: People who truly understand
feature1.description: You don't have to face this alone. Connect with people who truly understand—because they're going through it too.

feature2.title: Anonymous and safe
feature2.description: No real name or photo needed. A safe space to speak honestly. Slip up? Start over? You'll find warmth, not judgment.

feature3.title: See your progress
feature3.description: Track your clean time. Each day you make it through becomes proof that you can keep going.

social-proof.activity: 150+ new posts are shared in the community every day

highlight1.quote: After 20+ years of addiction, I've been clean for 365 days.
highlight1.author: User

highlight2.quote: Therapy didn't work, but after finding this app, they stopped gambling completely. Our family is so grateful.
highlight2.author: User

reviews.title: What our users say — App Store ★4.8

review1.name: User
review1.message: After 20+ years of addictive behavior, I've now been clean for 365 days. Everyone on the app is positive about recovery, and unlike other social media, you can keep going without worrying about others.

review2.name: User
review2.message: My family member had a serious gambling addiction. Therapy had no effect, but after finding this app, they stopped gambling completely. We're so grateful as a family.

review3.name: User
review3.message: I couldn't even go 2 days without slot machines, but since starting this app I've gone 10+ days gambling-free. Other users are incredibly kind, and there's an unspoken commitment to beating addiction.

review4.name: User
review4.message: Even if you slip up and start over, there's a warm, supportive atmosphere. Some people are cutting back instead of quitting completely, and that's okay too. A wonderful community.

review5.name: User
review5.message: This app has a sense of safety. You can't open up unless you feel safe. In that sense, it's like an open support group.

review6.name: User
review6.message: Everyone is so warm, it's hard to believe it's free. This app helped me keep going. I highly recommend giving it a try!

how-it-works.title: Get started for free in 3 steps

step1.title: Download the app
step1.description: Free on the App Store and Google Play. Sign up anonymously — no real name needed.

step2.title: Choose your struggle
step2.description: Pick from 12+ categories like alcohol, gambling, binge eating, and smoking. Your clean-time counter starts automatically.

step3.title: Recover together
step3.description: Share how you feel, support others, and see you're not alone. Your recovery community is waiting.

problem.title: Sound familiar?

items.text: You want to stop, but you keep going back to the same habit
No one around you truly understands what you're going through
You've tried quitting alone and failed—and blamed yourself every time
Therapy or counseling alone isn't enough to fill the daily loneliness

problem.closing: You don't have to face this alone.

items.question: Is it free?
items.answer: Yes. Posting, browsing the timeline, and the clean-time counter are all free. No hidden fees.

items.question: Can I stay anonymous?
items.answer: Absolutely. No real name or photo required. Just pick a nickname and you're ready to go.

items.question: What addictions are supported?
items.answer: We support many categories including alcohol, gambling, binge eating, smoking, social media, gaming, shopping, porn, drugs, caffeine, and codependency.

items.question: Can I take a break and come back?
items.answer: Of course. Go at your own pace. You can reset your counter anytime and start fresh whenever you're ready.

final-cta.title: Take the first step with others by your side
final-cta.description: With QuitMate, your will to quit becomes the strength to keep going.
final-cta.closing: Scan the QR code with your phone to download

learn.title: Learn | QuitMate
learn.description: A guide to addiction recovery. The knowledge and skills needed to recover, written so that people in recovery can read it on their own.
learn.hero-title: Learn how to recover from addiction
learn.hero-description: A practical guide covering the knowledge and skills needed for recovery, written so people in recovery can read it on their own.

gambling.title: Recovering from Gambling Addiction | QuitMate
gambling.description: A guide to recovering from gambling addiction (pachinko, pachislot, horse/boat/cycle racing, online casinos, sports betting, FX, etc.).
gambling.name: Recovering from Gambling Addiction
gambling.tagline: A practical guide for people in recovery, ordered by what works
gambling.intro: A guide covering the knowledge and skills needed to recover from gambling addiction (pachinko, pachislot, horse/boat/cycle racing, online casinos, sports betting, FX, etc.). Written so people in recovery can read it on their own.
gambling.phase-progress-notice: The first part, Big picture & emergency response (26 chapters), is currently available. The remaining four parts are being written and will be released as they are completed. This first part alone is designed to give you the first foothold for recovery.
gambling.usage-guide-intro: There is no single right way to read this book. Pick the pattern that fits your situation.
gambling.usage-pattern-a-body: Start from Chapter 1 and read in order. The book is designed so that reading sequentially lets your understanding build up step by step. One chapter a day or one a week is fine — go at your own pace.
gambling.usage-pattern-b-title: Pattern B: Read only the two chapters marked Important
gambling.usage-pattern-b-body: If you have no time, or if you are in crisis, just these two chapters are enough to start: Chapter 7 (Cut off access to money) and Chapter 9 (When you want to die: a safety plan).
gambling.usage-pattern-c-body: Pick chapters based on what you face right now: cravings, payday anxiety, suicidal thoughts, family conversations, debt fears.
gambling.usage-pattern-d-title: Pattern D: Read only the first part
gambling.usage-pattern-d-body: The first part alone (26 chapters) is enough to set up the first foothold of recovery. The later parts can wait until you have room.
gambling.disclaimer-body: This book covers general knowledge and is not a substitute for individual medical or legal advice. For specific treatment plans or debt relief, consult professionals (psychiatrists, addiction specialists, lawyers, etc.).

blog-app-promo.title: Together, you can quit
blog-app-promo.description: Connect anonymously with others who share your struggle. Whether it's alcohol, smoking, gambling, or other habits, you're not alone.

apps.title: Other apps | QuitMate

apps.heading: Other apps
apps.subheading: We offer apps tailored to different types of dependency.

common.title: Alcohol-Free Week | Build a Sober Habit with Daily Tasks
common.description: Learn, share, and track — 3 daily tasks to build a lasting sober habit. Science-based program with community support to help you quit drinking. Free on iOS and Android.

hero.title: 3 daily tasks to build a sober habit
hero.description: Learn, share, and track. A science-based daily program and supportive community help you quit drinking — one day at a time.

intro.title: Challenge yourself to quit alcohol for just one week
intro.description: Deciding to never drink alcohol again is tough, but doesn't 'just one week' sound more manageable? Review your daily alcohol intake and support your 7-day journey to reset your mind and body.

feature1.title: Daily tasks
feature1.description: Learn → Share → Track. Complete 3 simple steps each day to build sobriety skills. Read science-based articles on how alcohol affects your brain and body, then check in to record your progress. Not just a tracker — it's a habit-changing program.

feature2.title: Community timeline
feature2.description: Share struggles and wins with others on the same journey. Likes and comments keep you motivated. Writing about what you learn helps the knowledge stick.

feature3.title: Progress tracking
feature3.description: Your sober streak is counted automatically. Log 'I made it today' with a quick check-in and look back at your progress anytime. Seeing the numbers grow builds real confidence.

testimonial1.name: Sobriety Community
testimonial1.title: This app changed everything
testimonial1.message: I started my journey to quit drinking and found this app. The support from others with similar experiences has made a huge difference. I'm so thankful.

testimonial2.name: Sobriety Community
testimonial2.title: Support even after relapse
testimonial2.message: Such a great app. Some people here have been sober for over 100 days. We support each other, share ideas, and keep moving forward together.

testimonial3.name: Sobriety Community
testimonial3.title: I wish I found it sooner
testimonial3.message: I really wish I had discovered this app earlier. But even now, finding it feels like such a gift. I'm so glad I did.

final-cta.title: Start your alcohol-free challenge today to regain your best health!
final-cta.description: What matters for protecting your health is not temporary restraint, but 'changing your habits.' This app is more than just a tracking tool—it creates an opportunity to change how you relate to alcohol.

intro.title: We support your decision to quit
